column3: Customer name, email, phone, delivery address, pin code, order history, payment status, Shopify customer ID, notes added by team
column5: Order processing, customer account management, order history lookup
column7: Collected directly from customer at checkout or account registration
column12: Shopify PCI DSS Level 1, TLS encryption in transit, 2FA on admin account
column14: Manual deletion via Shopify customer admin or Shopify API
column17: Shopify stores data in USA. Confirm DPA is in place via Shopify DPA page. Restrict admin access to essential team members only.

column3: Customer name, email, phone, tokenised card data, UPI ID, VPA, transaction records, refund records
column5: Payment processing, refund and dispute management
column7: Collected at checkout via Razorpay payment widget
column12: Razorpay PCI DSS Level 1, RBI regulated, TLS, 2FA on dashboard
column14: Cannot delete — regulatory hold. Document exception in inventory. Contact Razorpay compliance for specific requests.
column17: Regulatory retention means erasure requests cannot be fully honoured for payment records. Inform customers of this at the point of consent.

column3: Customer name, phone, delivery address, pin code, order ID, shipment tracking status, delivery partner details
column5: Order fulfilment, shipment tracking, return management
column7: Received from Shopify via Shiprocket integration (automated)
column9: Shiprocket (processor); sub-processors: Delhivery, Ekart, Xpressbees, DTDC
column13: 1 year from delivery or return completion
column14: Request deletion via Shiprocket admin dashboard. Sub-processor data deletion must be requested separately.
column17: Shiprocket passes customer name and phone to last-mile carriers. Confirm Shiprocket's DPA covers sub-processor obligations. DPA NOT yet confirmed — action required.

column3: Customer name, email address, purchase history, email engagement data (opens, clicks, bounces), marketing segments, unsubscribe status
column5: Email marketing campaigns, abandoned cart recovery, product announcements, post-purchase flows
column7: Imported from Shopify via native Klaviyo-Shopify integration
column12: Klaviyo SOC 2 Type II, TLS encryption, 2FA
column13: 2 years from last engagement, or until unsubscribed
column14: Delete profile via Klaviyo dashboard or Klaviyo API. Unsubscribes must be honoured immediately — suppression list managed by Klaviyo.
column17: Cross-border transfer to USA. Requires DPA — download Klaviyo standard DPA from Klaviyo legal page. Disclose in privacy policy.

column3: Anonymised IP address, device type, browser, city-level location, session data, pages visited, product views, conversion events, UTM parameters
column5: Website traffic analysis, UX optimisation, marketing attribution, conversion tracking
column7: Collected automatically via GA4 JavaScript tracking code on website
column12: IP anonymisation feature enabled (verify), Google TLS, Google Workspace security
column14: Use GA4 Data Deletion Request tool in GA4 Admin. Individual-level deletion available.
column16: Possible — if product is for children
column17: Implement cookie consent banner before loading GA4. Verify IP anonymisation is enabled in GA4 settings. If you sell products for children review Section 9 of DPDP Act.

column3: Hashed email addresses and phone numbers (custom audience upload), pixel-collected behavioural data (device ID, page views, add to cart, purchase events)
column5: Paid advertising, retargeting, lookalike audience creation
column7: Hashed data uploaded from Shopify export; pixel data collected via Meta Pixel on website
column12: Meta standard security, hashing before upload, Meta Business Privacy Policy
column13: Until custom audience is deleted, or until account closure
column14: Delete custom audiences directly in Meta Ads Manager. Pixel data retained by Meta per their policy.
column17: Customer consent required before uploading hashed data to Meta. Disclose Meta Pixel and custom audiences in privacy policy and cookie notice.

column3: Customer name, email, phone, order details shared in support chat, issue description, resolution notes, conversation history
column5: Customer support, issue resolution, complaint management
column7: Customers initiate contact via chat widget, email, or WhatsApp
column14: Delete contact and conversation via platform admin. Export data before deletion if needed for audit.
column17: Update platform name in this row to reflect your actual tool. Confirm DPA with your specific platform.

column3: Customer name, phone number, WhatsApp chat transcripts, order-related messages, support queries
column5: Customer support, order updates, delivery notifications
column7: Customer-initiated messages to your WhatsApp Business number
column12: WhatsApp end-to-end encryption for messages in transit
column14: Manual deletion of individual chats. No bulk deletion available in WhatsApp Business.
column17: No automated deletion available. Set a manual quarterly review. WhatsApp data cannot be exported or deleted at scale — evaluate whether WhatsApp is appropriate for support workflows at your stage.

column3: User ID, registered name, email, phone, hashed password, subscription status, order references, feature usage logs, session tokens
column5: Product functionality, account management, customer lookup, debugging
column9: Cloud provider (AWS / GCP / Azure) as infrastructure
column12: Encryption at rest (AES-256), TLS in transit, role-based access control (RBAC), access logs
column13: Duration of account + 1 year post-deletion request
column14: Automated via account deletion API endpoint or manual DB query with logged audit trail
column17: Confirm database region. If using AWS, ensure Mumbai (ap-south-1) region to avoid cross-border transfer. Review who has database access — restrict to engineering lead and founder only.

column3: Employee name, phone, personal email, home address, date of birth, PAN number, Aadhaar number, bank account and IFSC, salary, designation, date of joining, leave records, emergency contact
column5: Payroll processing, HR management, statutory compliance (PF, ESI, TDS)
column7: Collected directly from employee during onboarding
column9: External accountant (via email or shared drive)
column12: Google Workspace access controls (if using Sheets), access restricted to 2 people maximum
column13: Duration of employment + 5 years post-exit (statutory requirement)
column14: Manual deletion from all copies including emailed attachments. Document deletion in writing.
column17: HIGHEST RISK ROW in this inventory. PAN and Aadhaar are sensitive data. Do not store in unencrypted spreadsheets. Move to a dedicated HR tool (Keka, Zoho Payroll, or similar). Stop sharing via email attachments.

column1: Add more rows above this line
1. Add consent checkbox at Shopify checkout — most D2C stores are missing this (DPDP Act Section 6)
2. Issue DPAs with Shiprocket and Klaviyo — these are the two most commonly unaddressed processors
3. Move HR data out of Google Sheets and into a dedicated tool with proper access controls
4. Implement a cookie consent banner for GA4 and Meta Pixel
5. Document the process for responding to customer erasure requests (Section 12) — test it before a request arrives
